label: claude CLI (claude-sonnet-4-6, --no-session-persistence)
source: manual — measured in chat session, quality from code review
notes: Claude CLI uses a ~11k token system prompt (vs agent-flow ~4.3k). 1h TTL cache by default. Does not always write files to disk for simple tasks — LRU and rate limiter outputs were inline chat text only. EventEmitter created a full project directory.

C1_lruCache.qualityNotes: Not written to disk — code reviewed as inline text. 10,731 output tokens suggests very complete implementation with 8+ test suites. Unable to verify compile/run.

C2_rateLimiter.qualityNotes: Not written to disk. 3 turns is notably efficient. Quality inferred as high from output token count.

C3_eventEmitter.qualityNotes: Written to disk at ~/event-emitter/. Uses _onceWrappers secondary Map for once+off. EventMap=object (loose). on() deduplicates (non-Node-standard). emit() always requires data arg — no void event support. Added removeAllListeners(). 5 well-structured tests with throw-based harness.

overallQuality.basis: EventEmitter only (only task written to disk). LRU and rate limiter not reviewable.
weaknesses: No void event support in emit()
on() deduplication diverges from Node.js standard
